biodiversityActNote: Regulated under Indian Biological Diversity Act, 2002. Commercial harvesting requires prior approval from National Biodiversity Authority.
harvestRestriction: Government-controlled harvesting only in Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala

biodiversityActNote: Listed as Critically Endangered. International trade requires CITES permit.
harvestRestriction: Wild harvesting prohibited. Plantation cultivation allowed with permits.

biodiversityActNote: Protected under Wildlife Protection Act. Export of raw material banned.
harvestRestriction: Collection from wild prohibited without government license

biodiversityActNote: Critically endangered alpine herb. Included in negative list for export.
harvestRestriction: Wild collection banned. Cultivation being promoted in Uttarakhand.

biodiversityActNote: High-altitude Himalayan species under severe collection pressure.
harvestRestriction: Regulated harvesting. Commercial collection requires forest department permit.

biodiversityActNote: Source of taxol (anti-cancer). Protected under multiple Indian and international laws.
harvestRestriction: Bark stripping and felling prohibited. Only fallen/pruned material permitted.

biodiversityActNote: Himalayan orchid used in Unani medicine. Severely over-collected.
harvestRestriction: Collection completely prohibited in most states

biodiversityActNote: Listed as Critically Endangered by IUCN. Rajasthan populations severely depleted.
harvestRestriction: Wild tapping banned in most states. Only plantation-sourced material permitted.

biodiversityActNote: Regulated due to commercial demand for colchicine extraction.
harvestRestriction: Wild collection restricted. Cultivation encouraged in Tamil Nadu and Odisha.

biodiversityActNote: Over-harvested from Eastern Himalayas. Included in priority species for conservation.
harvestRestriction: Sustainable harvesting protocols mandatory in Sikkim and Darjeeling.
